_comment: Example page config for a DIFFERENT sub-brand/tour. Demonstrates (a) theming via config.theme, and (b) conditional sections — this page omits the video, countdown and brochure, so those sections simply do not render. Inject in production with:  <script>window.__PAGE_CONFIG = <this object> </script>

hero.subhead: A guided, structured immersion into Bali's ecology, craft and community — built for teenagers, designed for parents' peace of mind.

valueCards.title: Ecological Literacy
valueCards.body: Coral reefs, rice terraces and conservation up close.

valueCards.title: Cultural Respect
valueCards.body: Temples, craft villages and homestay etiquette.

valueCards.title: Teamwork
valueCards.body: Group challenges that build trust and initiative.

valueCards.title: Guided Independence
valueCards.body: Freedom to explore within a safe framework.

hero.imageSubtitle: Ecology, Culture and Community — A Structured Journey

intro.title: Learning That Doesn't Fit in a Classroom.
paragraphs: Some lessons can only be learned in the field — balancing on a reef survey line, or learning a craft from someone who has practised it for forty years.
Bali offers a compact, safe and astonishingly diverse setting for exactly that kind of growth.
intro.gainsQuote: They don't just visit Bali. They observe ecosystems, practise crafts, and learn how communities sustain themselves.

highlights.title: Field Highlights
highlights.subtitle: Each region is chosen for a specific learning outcome.

cards.name: Ubud
cards.frontBody: Craft villages, the Monkey Forest and a working art studio.
cards.backBody: Students learn woodcarving and batik from master artisans, then reflect on craft as discipline.

cards.name: Sidemen
cards.frontBody: Subak rice-terrace irrigation — a 1,000-year-old cooperative water system.
cards.backBody: A guided walk explains how an entire valley shares water fairly. A living lesson in systems and fairness.
cards.benefit: Systems thinking in the real world.

cards.name: Amed
cards.frontBody: Snorkel reef surveys with a marine biologist.
cards.backBody: Students record species counts on dive slates and contribute to a local reef-health dataset.
cards.benefit: Science is something you do, not just read.

highlights.bannerBody: See the full day-by-day plan and supervision model.

safety.title: Safe by Design.
safety.subtitle: Every activity is risk-assessed; supervision is constant.

stats.title: Staff Ratio
stats.body: Trained field leaders plus a registered nurse.

safety.includedQuote: Adventure within structure. Freedom within supervision.
included: Reef survey and craft workshop fees

items.name: Meera D.
items.quote: The communication before and during the trip was outstanding. I never once worried.

items.name: Sanjay R.
items.quote: My son came home talking about coral reefs and water-sharing systems for weeks.

investment.title: Transparent Pricing
investment.subtitle: A simple two-instalment plan. No hidden costs.
investment.note: A detailed cost sheet is shared on enquiry.

register.title: Register Your Interest — Bali 2026
register.intro: Tell us about the participant and we'll be in touch within one business day.
register.successBody: Our team will reach out within one business day with the full itinerary and next steps.

covers.title: Q&A
covers.body: A direct line to our programme team.

faqs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqs.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Bali 2026

items.a: Seven days across three regions of Bali — Ubud, Sidemen and Amed.
Students aged 14 to 18, grouped by age band.
One trained field leader for every eight students, plus a registered nurse on the trip.
Yes — comprehensive travel insurance and a 24/7 on-call line for parents.

items.q: How do I secure a place?
items.a: Submit the registration form; a team member confirms availability and next steps within one business day.
